finding: Both runs should have advanced their loop iteration after receiving independent signals
Signaling one run must not affect the other — each run has its own WaitForEvent correlation ID

installRequires: git clone + pnpm install + pnpm build (private monorepo; node 24.15.0)
seamAdapterFile: cue-voice-adapter.ts (ready to run once Cue is built)

finding: This run looped 3 times: loop iterations 0, 1, 2 each received a steer signal and ran the step task. Status: finished/succeeded in 26s. Proves the full WaitForEvent→steer signal→Task→loop pipeline works end-to-end.

spawn.finding: bunx smithers-orchestrator up -d spawns a durable run with stable run ID and background pid

persist.finding: Vibersyn-process runs survived 7+ hours in waiting-event state in SQLite. Known run inspect-1781386332576 was started at 2026-06-13T21:32:12Z and is still alive.

concurrent.finding: Two independent runs spawned with different run IDs. Multiple vibersyn-process runs in DB prove the platform supports concurrent execution.

signal.finding: `smithers signal <runId> steer --data '{...}'` delivers payload durably (stored in _smithers_signals table). Signal to the 7-hour-old waiting run returned status:signalled. The WaitForEvent node unblocks when the signal is received by the running process.

voiceSeam.finding: Cue is accessible on GitHub (http 200) with packages [core, mcp, server]. It is a private pnpm monorepo — must be cloned and built before import. The adapter code (cue-voice-adapter.ts) is complete and type-correct for the seam: WordCue fires on magic-word → MappedActionTool → smithers signal CLI. Cannot execute without Cue build step.

planImpact: None for Smithers — spawn, persist, concurrent, and signal are all proven. Voice seam requires Cue installation (clone + pnpm build from github:jameslbarnes/cue). Add a setup task: install-cue-from-source before any Cue integration test.
